revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「御櫛神社(みくしじんじゃ)は、[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]椹原にある式内社。 == 概要 == 大字椹原の集落から堂の谷を登りつめると、頂上に近い右側の丘陵上に南面して鎮座する。 [[延喜式]]にもみえる古社であるが、創祀や由緒は詳かでない。 [[祭神]]は、[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]。 向かって右側の、境内若宮社は、祭神、天押雲命…」
revision.text:
御櫛神社(みくしじんじゃ)は、[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]椹原にある式内社。
== 概要 == 大字椹原の集落から堂の谷を登りつめると、頂上に近い右側の丘陵上に南面して鎮座する。
[[延喜式]]にもみえる古社であるが、創祀や由緒は詳かでない。
[[祭神]]は、[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]。
向かって右側の、境内若宮社は、祭神、天押雲命。
本殿の玉垣外側にある、境内頭瘡社は。明細帳に「祭神不詳或云大穴智命」とある。
その左丘陵上の八王子社は、御神体は自然石で、同明細帳では「祭神不詳或云八柱神」とある<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{DEFAULTSORT:みくしじんじゃ}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:平群町の建築物]] [[Category:平群町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] <references />{{Shinto-stub}}

御櫛神社(みくしじんじゃ)は、[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]椹原にある式内社。
== 概要 == 大字椹原の集落から堂の谷を登りつめると、頂上に近い右側の丘陵上に南面して鎮座する。
[[延喜式]]にもみえる古社であるが、創祀や由緒は詳かでない。
[[祭神]]は、[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]。
向かって右側の、境内若宮社は、祭神、[[天押雲根命|天押雲命]]。
本殿の玉垣外側にある、境内頭瘡社は。神社明細帳に「祭神不詳或云(あるいは)大穴智命」とある。
その左丘陵上の八王子社は、御神体は自然石で、同明細帳では「祭神不詳或云八柱神」とある<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{DEFAULTSORT:みくしじんじゃ}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:平群町の建築物]] [[Category:平群町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] <references />{{Shinto-stub}}

== 概要 == 大字椹原の集落から堂の谷を登りつめると、頂上に近い右側の[[丘陵]]上に南面して[[鎮座]]する。
[[延喜式]]にもみえる古社であるが、創祀や由緒は詳かでない。
[[祭神]]は、[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]。
但し、古記録には、[[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉比咩]]、[[玉櫛媛|玉櫛姫]]等の名が見え、中世に主祭神が変わったとみられる。
向かって右側の境内若宮社は、祭神、[[天押雲根命|天押雲命]]。
本殿の玉垣外側にある境内頭瘡社は、[[神社明細帳]]に「祭神不詳或云（あるいは）大穴智命」とある。
その左丘陵上の八王子社は、御神体は自然石で、同明細帳では「祭神不詳或云八柱神」とある<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>。

本殿の玉垣外側にある境内頭瘡社は、[[神社明細帳]]に「祭神不詳或云（あるいは）大穴智命」とある。

本殿扉前浜床には、この地域では珍しい土製の狛犬がおかれてある。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references />
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みくししんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:平群町の建築物]] [[Category:平群町の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]]

{{神社 | 名称 = 御櫛神社 | 画像 = | 所在地 = [[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]椹原493番地 | ISO = JP-29 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 37 | 緯度秒 = 0.4 | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 41 | 経度秒 = 25.4 | 祭神 = [[天児屋命|天児屋根命]] | 神体 = | 社格 = | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 札所等 = | 宮司 = | 別名 = | 例祭 = | 神事 = | 地図 = Japan Nara }} '''御櫛神社'''（みくしじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]椹原に[[鎮座]]する[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]。[[旧社格]]は、[[村社]]。
== 概要 == 大字椹原(ふしはら)の集落から堂の谷を登りつめると、頂上に近い右側の[[丘陵]]上に南面して鎮座する。
[[延喜式]]にもみえる古社であるが、創祀や由緒は詳かでない。
但し、立地場所から村の後方の谷沿いの、農業用水を守る神として祀られたという説がある。
[[祭神]]は、[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]。
但し、日本惣国風土記には、[[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉比咩]]、[[特選神名牒|特選神名帳]]には、[[玉櫛媛|玉櫛姫]]の名が見え、中世に主祭神が変わったとみられる。
向かって右側の境内社は、祭神、[[天押雲根命|天押雲命]]<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>。
本殿の玉垣外側にある境内社は、[[神社明細帳]]に「祭神不詳或云（あるいは）大穴智命」とある。
その左丘陵上の八王子社は、御神体は自然石で、同明細帳では「祭神不詳或云八柱神」とある<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>。
本殿扉前浜床には、この地域では珍しい土製の狛犬がおかれてある。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references />
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みくししんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:平群町の建築物]] [[Category:平群町の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]]

{{nihongo|'''Mikushi Shrine'''|御櫛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mikushi Shrine'''|御櫛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mikushi Shrine'''|御櫛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mikushi Shrine'''|御櫛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mikushi Shrine'''|御櫛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mikushi Shrine'''|御櫛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

revision.comment: 21 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称 = 御櫛神社 | 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=生駒郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | 所在地 = [[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]椹原493番地 | ISO = JP-29 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 37 | 緯度秒 = 0.4 | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 41 | 経度秒 = 25.4 | 祭神 = [[天児屋命|天児屋根命]] | 神体 = | 社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 札所等 = | 宮司 = | 別名 = | 例祭 = | 神事 = | 地図 = Japan Nara }} '''御櫛神社'''（みくしじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]椹原に[[鎮座]]する[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]。[[旧社格]]は、[[村社]]。
== 概要 == 大字椹原(ふしはら)の集落から堂の谷を登りつめると、頂上に近い右側の[[丘陵]]上に南面して鎮座する。
[[延喜式]]にもみえる古社であるが、創祀や由緒は詳かでない。
但し、立地場所から村の後方の谷沿いの、農業用水を守る神として祀られたという説がある。
[[祭神]]は、[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]。
但し、日本惣国風土記には、[[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉比咩]]、[[特選神名牒|特選神名帳]]には、[[玉櫛媛|玉櫛姫]]の名が見え、中世に主祭神が変わったとみられる。
向かって右側の境内社は、祭神、[[天押雲根命|天押雲命]]<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>。
本殿の玉垣外側にある境内社は、[[神社明細帳]]に「祭神不詳或云（あるいは）大穴智命」とある。
その左丘陵上の八王子社は、御神体は自然石で、同明細帳では「祭神不詳或云八柱神」とある<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>。
本殿扉前浜床には、この地域では珍しい土製の狛犬がおかれてある。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references />
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みくししんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:平群町の建築物]] [[Category:平群町の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[御櫛神社]] to [[Mikushi Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Merging Japanese Wikipedia import with English content
revision.text:

向かって右側の境内社は、祭神、[[天押雲根命|天押雲命]]<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>。
本殿の玉垣外側にある境内社は、[[神社明細帳]]に「祭神不詳或云（あるいは）大穴智命」とある。
その左丘陵上の八王子社は、御神体は自然石で、同明細帳では「祭神不詳或云八柱神」とある<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>。
本殿扉前浜床には、この地域では珍しい土製の狛犬がおかれてある。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references />
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みくししんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:平群町の建築物]] [[Category:平群町の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]]

== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 | 名称 = 御櫛神社 | 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=生駒郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | 所在地 = [[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]椹原493番地 | ISO = JP-29 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 37 | 緯度秒 = 0.4 | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 41 | 経度秒 = 25.4 | 祭神 = [[天児屋命|天児屋根命]] | 神体 = | 社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 札所等 = | 宮司 = | 別名 = | 例祭 = | 神事 = | 地図 = Japan Nara }} '''御櫛神社'''（みくしじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]椹原に[[鎮座]]する[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]。[[旧社格]]は、[[村社]]。
== 概要 == 大字椹原(ふしはら)の集落から堂の谷を登りつめると、頂上に近い右側の[[丘陵]]上に南面して鎮座する。
[[延喜式]]にもみえる古社であるが、創祀や由緒は詳かでない。
但し、立地場所から村の後方の谷沿いの、農業用水を守る神として祀られたという説がある。
[[祭神]]は、[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]。
但し、日本惣国風土記には、[[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉比咩]]、[[特選神名牒|特選神名帳]]には、[[玉櫛媛|玉櫛姫]]の名が見え、中世に主祭神が変わったとみられる。
向かって右側の境内社は、祭神、[[天押雲根命|天押雲命]]<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>。
本殿の玉垣外側にある境内社は、[[神社明細帳]]に「祭神不詳或云（あるいは）大穴智命」とある。
その左丘陵上の八王子社は、御神体は自然石で、同明細帳では「祭神不詳或云八柱神」とある<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>。
本殿扉前浜床には、この地域では珍しい土製の狛犬がおかれてある。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references />
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みくししんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:平群町の建築物]] [[Category:平群町の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]]
==End Japanese==

[[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]]
[[JA:御櫛神社]]
{{wikidata link|Q119929667}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]
{{translated page|ja|御櫛神社|version=106926865|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 | 名称 = 御櫛神社 | 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=生駒郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | 所在地 = [[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]椹原493番地 | ISO = JP-29 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 37 | 緯度秒 = 0.4 | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 41 | 経度秒 = 25.4 | 祭神 = [[天児屋命|天児屋根命]] | 神体 = | 社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 札所等 = | 宮司 = | 別名 = | 例祭 = | 神事 = | 地図 = Japan Nara }} '''御櫛神社'''（みくしじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]椹原に[[鎮座]]する[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]。[[旧社格]]は、[[村社]]。
== 概要 == 大字椹原(ふしはら)の集落から堂の谷を登りつめると、頂上に近い右側の[[丘陵]]上に南面して鎮座する。
[[延喜式]]にもみえる古社であるが、創祀や由緒は詳かでない。
但し、立地場所から村の後方の谷沿いの、農業用水を守る神として祀られたという説がある。
[[祭神]]は、[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]。
但し、日本惣国風土記には、[[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉比咩]]、[[特選神名牒|特選神名帳]]には、[[玉櫛媛|玉櫛姫]]の名が見え、中世に主祭神が変わったとみられる。
向かって右側の境内社は、祭神、[[天押雲根命|天押雲命]]<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>。
本殿の玉垣外側にある境内社は、[[神社明細帳]]に「祭神不詳或云（あるいは）大穴智命」とある。
その左丘陵上の八王子社は、御神体は自然石で、同明細帳では「祭神不詳或云八柱神」とある<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>。
本殿扉前浜床には、この地域では珍しい土製の狛犬がおかれてある。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references />
{{Shinto-stub}}
==End Japanese== [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:平群町の建築物]] [[Category:平群町の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]]

{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}にもみえる古社であるが、創祀や由緒は詳かでない。
但し、立地場所から村の後方の谷沿いの、農業用水を守る神として祀られたという説がある。
{{jalink|祭神|Q11591100}}は、{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508|天児屋根命}}。
但し、日本惣国風土記には、{{jalink|トヨタマヒメ|Q2329261|豊玉比咩}}、{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092|特選神名帳}}には、{{jalink|玉櫛媛|Q11572681|玉櫛姫}}の名が見え、中世に主祭神が変わったとみられる。
向かって右側の境内社は、祭神、{{jalink|天押雲根命|Q91088537|天押雲命}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>。
本殿の玉垣外側にある境内社は、{{jalink|神社明細帳|Q11590704}}に「祭神不詳或云（あるいは）大穴智命」とある。
その左丘陵上の八王子社は、御神体は自然石で、同明細帳では「祭神不詳或云八柱神」とある<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>。
本殿扉前浜床には、この地域では珍しい土製の狛犬がおかれてある。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references />
{{Shinto-stub}}
==End Japanese== [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:平群町の建築物]] [[Category:平群町の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]]

[[JA:御櫛神社]]

== Japanese content ==
{{神社

| 画像    = {{画像募集中|cat=生駒郡}}

'''御櫛神社'''（みくしじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|生駒郡|Q971003}}{{jalink|平群町|Q1346790}}椹原に{{jalink|鎮座}}する{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}。{{jalink|旧社格}}は、{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。

=== 概要 ===
大字椹原(ふしはら)の集落から堂の谷を登りつめると、頂上に近い右側の{{jalink|丘陵|Q222912}}上に南面して鎮座する。

{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}にもみえる古社であるが、創祀や由緒は詳かでない。

但し、立地場所から村の後方の谷沿いの、農業用水を守る神として祀られたという説がある。

{{jalink|祭神|Q11591100}}は、{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508|天児屋根命}}。

但し、日本惣国風土記には、{{jalink|トヨタマヒメ|Q2329261|豊玉比咩}}、{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092|特選神名帳}}には、{{jalink|玉櫛媛|Q11572681|玉櫛姫}}の名が見え、中世に主祭神が変わったとみられる。

向かって右側の境内社は、祭神、{{jalink|天押雲根命|Q91088537|天押雲命}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>。

本殿の玉垣外側にある境内社は、{{jalink|神社明細帳|Q11590704}}に「祭神不詳或云（あるいは）大穴智命」とある。

その左丘陵上の八王子社は、御神体は自然石で、同明細帳では「祭神不詳或云八柱神」とある<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>。

本殿扉前浜床には、この地域では珍しい土製の狛犬がおかれてある。

=== 脚注 ===
{{脚注ヘルプ}}

{{nihongo|'''Mikushi Shrine'''|御櫛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''御櫛神社'''（みくしじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|生駒郡|Q971003}}{{jalink|平群町|Q1346790}}椹原に{{jalink|鎮座}}する{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}。{{jalink|旧社格}}は、{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。

=== 概要 ===
大字椹原(ふしはら)の集落から堂の谷を登りつめると、頂上に近い右側の{{jalink|丘陵|Q222912}}上に南面して鎮座する。

{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}にもみえる古社であるが、創祀や由緒は詳かでない。

但し、立地場所から村の後方の谷沿いの、農業用水を守る神として祀られたという説がある。

{{jalink|祭神|Q11591100}}は、{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508|天児屋根命}}。

但し、日本惣国風土記には、{{jalink|トヨタマヒメ|Q2329261|豊玉比咩}}、{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092|特選神名帳}}には、{{jalink|玉櫛媛|Q11572681|玉櫛姫}}の名が見え、中世に主祭神が変わったとみられる。

向かって右側の境内社は、祭神、{{jalink|天押雲根命|Q91088537|天押雲命}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>。

本殿の玉垣外側にある境内社は、{{jalink|神社明細帳|Q11590704}}に「祭神不詳或云（あるいは）大穴智命」とある。

その左丘陵上の八王子社は、御神体は自然石で、同明細帳では「祭神不詳或云八柱神」とある<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>。

本殿扉前浜床には、この地域では珍しい土製の狛犬がおかれてある。

=== 脚注 ===

{{nihongo|'''Mikushi Shrine'''|御櫛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''御櫛神社'''（みくしじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|生駒郡|Q971003}}{{jalink|平群町|Q1346790}}椹原に{{jalink|鎮座}}する{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}。{{jalink|旧社格}}は、{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。

=== 概要 ===
大字椹原(ふしはら)の集落から堂の谷を登りつめると、頂上に近い右側の{{jalink|丘陵|Q222912}}上に南面して鎮座する。

{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}にもみえる古社であるが、創祀や由緒は詳かでない。

但し、立地場所から村の後方の谷沿いの、農業用水を守る神として祀られたという説がある。

{{jalink|祭神|Q11591100}}は、{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508|天児屋根命}}。

但し、日本惣国風土記には、{{jalink|トヨタマヒメ|Q2329261|豊玉比咩}}、{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092|特選神名帳}}には、{{jalink|玉櫛媛|Q11572681|玉櫛姫}}の名が見え、中世に主祭神が変わったとみられる。

向かって右側の境内社は、祭神、{{jalink|天押雲根命|Q91088537|天押雲命}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>。

本殿の玉垣外側にある境内社は、{{jalink|神社明細帳|Q11590704}}に「祭神不詳或云（あるいは）大穴智命」とある。

その左丘陵上の八王子社は、御神体は自然石で、同明細帳では「祭神不詳或云八柱神」とある<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>。

本殿扉前浜床には、この地域では珍しい土製の狛犬がおかれてある。

== Japanese content ==
{{神社

| 画像    = {{画像募集中|cat=生駒郡}}

'''御櫛神社'''（みくしじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|生駒郡|Q971003}}{{jalink|平群町|Q1346790}}椹原に{{jalink|鎮座}}する{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}。{{jalink|旧社格}}は、{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。

=== 概要 ===
大字椹原(ふしはら)の集落から堂の谷を登りつめると、頂上に近い右側の{{jalink|丘陵|Q222912}}上に南面して鎮座する。

{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}にもみえる古社であるが、創祀や由緒は詳かでない。

但し、立地場所から村の後方の谷沿いの、農業用水を守る神として祀られたという説がある。

{{jalink|祭神|Q11591100}}は、{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508|天児屋根命}}。

但し、日本惣国風土記には、{{jalink|トヨタマヒメ|Q2329261|豊玉比咩}}、{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092|特選神名帳}}には、{{jalink|玉櫛媛|Q11572681|玉櫛姫}}の名が見え、中世に主祭神が変わったとみられる。

向かって右側の境内社は、祭神、{{jalink|天押雲根命|Q91088537|天押雲命}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>。

本殿の玉垣外側にある境内社は、{{jalink|神社明細帳|Q11590704}}に「祭神不詳或云（あるいは）大穴智命」とある。

その左丘陵上の八王子社は、御神体は自然石で、同明細帳では「祭神不詳或云八柱神」とある<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>。

本殿扉前浜床には、この地域では珍しい土製の狛犬がおかれてある。

=== 脚注 ===

{{nihongo|'''Mikushi Shrine'''|御櫛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社

'''御櫛神社'''（みくしじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|生駒郡|Q971003}}{{jalink|平群町|Q1346790}}椹原に{{jalink|鎮座}}する{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}。{{jalink|旧社格}}は、{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。

=== 概要 ===
大字椹原(ふしはら)の集落から堂の谷を登りつめると、頂上に近い右側の{{jalink|丘陵|Q222912}}上に南面して鎮座する。

{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}にもみえる古社であるが、創祀や由緒は詳かでない。

但し、立地場所から村の後方の谷沿いの、農業用水を守る神として祀られたという説がある。

{{jalink|祭神|Q11591100}}は、{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508|天児屋根命}}。

但し、日本惣国風土記には、{{jalink|トヨタマヒメ|Q2329261|豊玉比咩}}、{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092|特選神名帳}}には、{{jalink|玉櫛媛|Q11572681|玉櫛姫}}の名が見え、中世に主祭神が変わったとみられる。

向かって右側の境内社は、祭神、{{jalink|天押雲根命|Q91088537|天押雲命}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>。

本殿の玉垣外側にある境内社は、{{jalink|神社明細帳|Q11590704}}に「祭神不詳或云（あるいは）大穴智命」とある。

その左丘陵上の八王子社は、御神体は自然石で、同明細帳では「祭神不詳或云八柱神」とある<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>。

本殿扉前浜床には、この地域では珍しい土製の狛犬がおかれてある。

=== 脚注 ===
{{脚注ヘルプ}}

{{nihongo|'''Mikushi Shrine'''|御櫛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''御櫛神社'''（みくしじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|生駒郡|Q971003}}{{jalink|平群町|Q1346790}}椹原に{{jalink|鎮座}}する{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}。{{jalink|旧社格}}は、{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。

=== 概要 ===
大字椹原(ふしはら)の集落から堂の谷を登りつめると、頂上に近い右側の{{jalink|丘陵|Q222912}}上に南面して鎮座する。

{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}にもみえる古社であるが、創祀や由緒は詳かでない。

但し、立地場所から村の後方の谷沿いの、農業用水を守る神として祀られたという説がある。

{{jalink|祭神|Q11591100}}は、{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508|天児屋根命}}。

但し、日本惣国風土記には、{{jalink|トヨタマヒメ|Q2329261|豊玉比咩}}、{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092|特選神名帳}}には、{{jalink|玉櫛媛|Q11572681|玉櫛姫}}の名が見え、中世に主祭神が変わったとみられる。

向かって右側の境内社は、祭神、{{jalink|天押雲根命|Q91088537|天押雲命}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>。

本殿の玉垣外側にある境内社は、{{jalink|神社明細帳|Q11590704}}に「祭神不詳或云（あるいは）大穴智命」とある。

その左丘陵上の八王子社は、御神体は自然石で、同明細帳では「祭神不詳或云八柱神」とある<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>。

本殿扉前浜床には、この地域では珍しい土製の狛犬がおかれてある。

=== 脚注 ===

{{nihongo|'''Mikushi Shrine'''|御櫛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
| 名称    = 御櫛神社
| 画像    = {{画像募集中|cat=生駒郡}}

'''御櫛神社'''（みくしじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|生駒郡|Q971003}}{{jalink|平群町|Q1346790}}椹原に{{jalink|鎮座}}する{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}。{{jalink|旧社格}}は、{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。

=== 概要 ===
大字椹原(ふしはら)の集落から堂の谷を登りつめると、頂上に近い右側の{{jalink|丘陵|Q222912}}上に南面して鎮座する。

{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}にもみえる古社であるが、創祀や由緒は詳かでない。

但し、立地場所から村の後方の谷沿いの、農業用水を守る神として祀られたという説がある。

{{jalink|祭神|Q11591100}}は、{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508|天児屋根命}}。

但し、日本惣国風土記には、{{jalink|トヨタマヒメ|Q2329261|豊玉比咩}}、{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092|特選神名帳}}には、{{jalink|玉櫛媛|Q11572681|玉櫛姫}}の名が見え、中世に主祭神が変わったとみられる。

向かって右側の境内社は、祭神、{{jalink|天押雲根命|Q91088537|天押雲命}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>。

本殿の玉垣外側にある境内社は、{{jalink|神社明細帳|Q11590704}}に「祭神不詳或云（あるいは）大穴智命」とある。

その左丘陵上の八王子社は、御神体は自然石で、同明細帳では「祭神不詳或云八柱神」とある<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>。

本殿扉前浜床には、この地域では珍しい土製の狛犬がおかれてある。

=== 脚注 ===
{{脚注ヘルプ}}

{{nihongo|'''Mikushi Shrine'''|御櫛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Mikushi Shrine''' (みくしじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} {{jalink|enshrined}} in Fushihara, {{jalink|Heguri|Q1346790}}, {{jalink|Ikoma District|Q971003}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. Its {{jalink|former shrine ranking}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}.

=== Overview ===
From the settlement of Ōaza Fushihara (ふしはら), if one climbs to the end of Dō no Tani (Hall Valley), the shrine is enshrined facing south on a {{jalink|hill|Q222912}} on the right side near the summit.

It is an ancient shrine also mentioned in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}, but the origins and history of its founding are not clear.

However, there is a theory that, based on its location along a valley behind the village, it was enshrined as a deity protecting agricultural water.

The {{jalink|enshrined deity|Q11591100}} is {{jalink|Ame no Koyane|Q2564508|Ame no Koyane}}.

However, in the Nihon Sōkoku Fudoki, the name {{jalink|Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime}} appears, and in the {{jalink|Tokusen Shinmyōchō|Q11571092|Tokusen Shinmyōchō}}, the name {{jalink|Tamakushihime|Q11572681|Tamakushihime}} appears, suggesting that the principal deity changed during the medieval period.

The auxiliary shrine on the right side of the grounds is dedicated to {{jalink|Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|Q91088537|Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

The auxiliary shrine outside the tamagaki (sacred fence) of the main hall is recorded in the {{jalink|Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704}} as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Ōnamuji no Mikoto."

The Hachiōji Shrine on the hill to the left has a natural stone as its sacred object, and the same Meisaichō records it as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Yahashira no Kami"<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

On the hamayuka (floor platform) in front of the main hall doors, there are clay komainu (guardian dogs), which are rare in this area.

=== Notes ===

'''Mikushi Shrine''' (みくしじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} {{jalink|enshrined}} in Fushihara, {{jalink|Heguri|Q1346790}}, {{jalink|Ikoma District|Q971003}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. Its {{jalink|former shrine ranking}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}.

=== Overview ===
From the settlement of Ōaza Fushihara (ふしはら), if one climbs to the end of Dō no Tani (Hall Valley), the shrine is enshrined facing south on a {{jalink|hill|Q222912}} on the right side near the summit.

It is an ancient shrine also mentioned in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}, but the origins and history of its founding are not clear.

However, there is a theory that, based on its location along a valley behind the village, it was enshrined as a deity protecting agricultural water.

The {{jalink|enshrined deity|Q11591100}} is {{jalink|Ame no Koyane|Q2564508|Ame no Koyane}}.

However, in the Nihon Sōkoku Fudoki, the name {{jalink|Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime}} appears, and in the {{jalink|Tokusen Shinmyōchō|Q11571092|Tokusen Shinmyōchō}}, the name {{jalink|Tamakushihime|Q11572681|Tamakushihime}} appears, suggesting that the principal deity changed during the medieval period.

The auxiliary shrine on the right side of the grounds is dedicated to {{jalink|Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|Q91088537|Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

The auxiliary shrine outside the tamagaki (sacred fence) of the main hall is recorded in the {{jalink|Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704}} as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Ōnamuji no Mikoto."

The Hachiōji Shrine on the hill to the left has a natural stone as its sacred object, and the same Meisaichō records it as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Yahashira no Kami"<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

On the hamayuka (floor platform) in front of the main hall doors, there are clay komainu (guardian dogs), which are rare in this area.

=== Notes ===

'''Mikushi Shrine''' (みくしじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} {{jalink|enshrined}} in Fushihara, {{jalink|Heguri|Q1346790}}, {{jalink|Ikoma District|Q971003}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. Its {{jalink|former shrine ranking}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}.

=== Overview ===
From the settlement of Ōaza Fushihara (ふしはら), if one climbs to the end of Dō no Tani (Hall Valley), the shrine is enshrined facing south on a {{jalink|hill|Q222912}} on the right side near the summit.

It is an ancient shrine also mentioned in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}, but the origins and history of its founding are not clear.

However, there is a theory that, based on its location along a valley behind the village, it was enshrined as a deity protecting agricultural water.

The {{jalink|enshrined deity|Q11591100}} is {{jalink|Ame no Koyane|Q2564508|Ame no Koyane}}.

However, in the Nihon Sōkoku Fudoki, the name {{jalink|Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime}} appears, and in the {{jalink|Tokusen Shinmyōchō|Q11571092|Tokusen Shinmyōchō}}, the name {{jalink|Tamakushihime|Q11572681|Tamakushihime}} appears, suggesting that the principal deity changed during the medieval period.

The auxiliary shrine on the right side of the grounds is dedicated to {{jalink|Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|Q91088537|Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

The auxiliary shrine outside the tamagaki (sacred fence) of the main hall is recorded in the {{jalink|Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704}} as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Ōnamuji no Mikoto."

The Hachiōji Shrine on the hill to the left has a natural stone as its sacred object, and the same Meisaichō records it as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Yahashira no Kami"<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

On the hamayuka (floor platform) in front of the main hall doors, there are clay komainu (guardian dogs), which are rare in this area.

=== Notes ===
{{脚注ヘルプ}}

'''Mikushi Shrine''' (みくしじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} {{jalink|enshrined}} in Fushihara, {{jalink|Heguri|Q1346790}}, {{jalink|Ikoma District|Q971003}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. Its {{jalink|former shrine ranking}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}.

=== Overview ===
From the settlement of Ōaza Fushihara (ふしはら), if one climbs to the end of Dō no Tani (Hall Valley), the shrine is enshrined facing south on a {{jalink|hill|Q222912}} on the right side near the summit.

It is an ancient shrine also mentioned in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}, but the origins and history of its founding are not clear.

However, there is a theory that, based on its location along a valley behind the village, it was enshrined as a deity protecting agricultural water.

The {{jalink|enshrined deity|Q11591100}} is {{jalink|Ame no Koyane|Q2564508|Ame no Koyane}}.

However, in the Nihon Sōkoku Fudoki, the name {{jalink|Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime}} appears, and in the {{jalink|Tokusen Shinmyōchō|Q11571092|Tokusen Shinmyōchō}}, the name {{jalink|Tamakushihime|Q11572681|Tamakushihime}} appears, suggesting that the principal deity changed during the medieval period.

The auxiliary shrine on the right side of the grounds is dedicated to {{jalink|Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|Q91088537|Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

The auxiliary shrine outside the tamagaki (sacred fence) of the main hall is recorded in the {{jalink|Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704}} as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Ōnamuji no Mikoto."

The Hachiōji Shrine on the hill to the left has a natural stone as its sacred object, and the same Meisaichō records it as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Yahashira no Kami"<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

On the hamayuka (floor platform) in front of the main hall doors, there are clay komainu (guardian dogs), which are rare in this area.

=== Notes ===
{{脚注ヘルプ}}

'''Mikushi Shrine''' (みくしじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} {{jalink|enshrined}} in Fushihara, {{jalink|Heguri|Q1346790}}, {{jalink|Ikoma District|Q971003}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. Its {{jalink|former shrine ranking}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}.

=== Overview ===
From the settlement of Ōaza Fushihara (ふしはら), if one climbs to the end of Dō no Tani (Hall Valley), the shrine is enshrined facing south on a {{jalink|hill|Q222912}} on the right side near the summit.

It is an ancient shrine also mentioned in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}, but the origins and history of its founding are not clear.

However, there is a theory that, based on its location along a valley behind the village, it was enshrined as a deity protecting agricultural water.

The {{jalink|enshrined deity|Q11591100}} is {{jalink|Ame no Koyane|Q2564508|Ame no Koyane}}.

However, in the Nihon Sōkoku Fudoki, the name {{jalink|Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime}} appears, and in the {{jalink|Tokusen Shinmyōchō|Q11571092|Tokusen Shinmyōchō}}, the name {{jalink|Tamakushihime|Q11572681|Tamakushihime}} appears, suggesting that the principal deity changed during the medieval period.

The auxiliary shrine on the right side of the grounds is dedicated to {{jalink|Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|Q91088537|Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

The auxiliary shrine outside the tamagaki (sacred fence) of the main hall is recorded in the {{jalink|Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704}} as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Ōnamuji no Mikoto."

The Hachiōji Shrine on the hill to the left has a natural stone as its sacred object, and the same Meisaichō records it as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Yahashira no Kami"<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

On the hamayuka (floor platform) in front of the main hall doors, there are clay komainu (guardian dogs), which are rare in this area.

'''Mikushi Shrine''' (みくしじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} {{jalink|enshrined}} in Fushihara, {{jalink|Heguri|Q1346790}}, {{jalink|Ikoma District|Q971003}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. Its {{jalink|former shrine ranking}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}.

=== Overview ===
From the settlement of Ōaza Fushihara (ふしはら), if one climbs to the end of Dō no Tani (Hall Valley), the shrine is enshrined facing south on a {{jalink|hill|Q222912}} on the right side near the summit.

It is an ancient shrine also mentioned in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}, but the origins and history of its founding are not clear.

However, there is a theory that, based on its location along a valley behind the village, it was enshrined as a deity protecting agricultural water.

The {{jalink|enshrined deity|Q11591100}} is {{jalink|Ame no Koyane|Q2564508|Ame no Koyane}}.

However, in the Nihon Sōkoku Fudoki, the name {{jalink|Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime}} appears, and in the {{jalink|Tokusen Shinmyōchō|Q11571092|Tokusen Shinmyōchō}}, the name {{jalink|Tamakushihime|Q11572681|Tamakushihime}} appears, suggesting that the principal deity changed during the medieval period.

The auxiliary shrine on the right side of the grounds is dedicated to {{jalink|Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|Q91088537|Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

The auxiliary shrine outside the tamagaki (sacred fence) of the main hall is recorded in the {{jalink|Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704}} as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Ōnamuji no Mikoto."

The Hachiōji Shrine on the hill to the left has a natural stone as its sacred object, and the same Meisaichō records it as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Yahashira no Kami"<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

On the hamayuka (floor platform) in front of the main hall doors, there are clay komainu (guardian dogs), which are rare in this area.

=== Notes ===

'''Mikushi Shrine''' (みくしじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} {{jalink|enshrined}} in Fushihara, {{jalink|Heguri|Q1346790}}, {{jalink|Ikoma District|Q971003}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. Its {{jalink|former shrine ranking}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}.

=== Overview ===
From the settlement of Ōaza Fushihara (ふしはら), if one climbs to the end of Dō no Tani (Hall Valley), the shrine is enshrined facing south on a {{jalink|hill|Q222912}} on the right side near the summit.

It is an ancient shrine also mentioned in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}, but the origins and history of its founding are not clear.

However, there is a theory that, based on its location along a valley behind the village, it was enshrined as a deity protecting agricultural water.

The {{jalink|enshrined deity|Q11591100}} is {{jalink|Ame no Koyane|Q2564508|Ame no Koyane}}.

However, in the Nihon Sōkoku Fudoki, the name {{jalink|Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime}} appears, and in the {{jalink|Tokusen Shinmyōchō|Q11571092|Tokusen Shinmyōchō}}, the name {{jalink|Tamakushihime|Q11572681|Tamakushihime}} appears, suggesting that the principal deity changed during the medieval period.

The auxiliary shrine on the right side of the grounds is dedicated to {{jalink|Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|Q91088537|Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

The auxiliary shrine outside the tamagaki (sacred fence) of the main hall is recorded in the {{jalink|Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704}} as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Ōnamuji no Mikoto."

The Hachiōji Shrine on the hill to the left has a natural stone as its sacred object, and the same Meisaichō records it as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Yahashira no Kami"<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

On the hamayuka (floor platform) in front of the main hall doors, there are clay komainu (guardian dogs), which are rare in this area.

=== Notes ===

{{nihongo|'''Mikushi Shrine'''|御櫛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Mikushi Shrine''' (みくしじんじゃ) is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} {{ill||lt=enshrined|UNKNOWN}} in Fushihara, {{ill|lt=Heguri|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790}}, {{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. Its {{ill||lt=former shrine ranking|UNKNOWN}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}.

=== Overview ===
From the settlement of Ōaza Fushihara (ふしはら), if one climbs to the end of Dō no Tani (Hall Valley), the shrine is enshrined facing south on a {{ill|lt=hill|Q222912|WD=Q222912}} on the right side near the summit.

It is an ancient shrine also mentioned in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}, but the origins and history of its founding are not clear.

However, there is a theory that, based on its location along a valley behind the village, it was enshrined as a deity protecting agricultural water.

The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100}} is {{ill|lt=Ame no Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame no Koyane}}.

However, in the Nihon Sōkoku Fudoki, the name {{ill|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime}} appears, and in the {{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō}}, the name {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime}} appears, suggesting that the principal deity changed during the medieval period.

The auxiliary shrine on the right side of the grounds is dedicated to {{ill|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|Q91088537|WD=Q91088537|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

The auxiliary shrine outside the tamagaki (sacred fence) of the main hall is recorded in the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704}} as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Ōnamuji no Mikoto."

The Hachiōji Shrine on the hill to the left has a natural stone as its sacred object, and the same Meisaichō records it as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Yahashira no Kami"<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

On the hamayuka (floor platform) in front of the main hall doors, there are clay komainu (guardian dogs), which are rare in this area.

=== Notes ===

'''Mikushi Shrine''' (みくしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|UNKNOWN}} in Fushihara, {{ill|lt=Heguri|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790}}, {{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine ranking|UNKNOWN}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}.

=== Overview ===
From the settlement of Ōaza Fushihara (ふしはら), if one climbs to the end of Dō no Tani (Hall Valley), the shrine is enshrined facing south on a {{ill|lt=hill|Q222912|WD=Q222912}} on the right side near the summit.

It is an ancient shrine also mentioned in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}, but the origins and history of its founding are not clear.

However, there is a theory that, based on its location along a valley behind the village, it was enshrined as a deity protecting agricultural water.

The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100}} is {{ill|lt=Ame no Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame no Koyane}}.

However, in the Nihon Sōkoku Fudoki, the name {{ill|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime}} appears, and in the {{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō}}, the name {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime}} appears, suggesting that the principal deity changed during the medieval period.

The auxiliary shrine on the right side of the grounds is dedicated to {{ill|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|Q91088537|WD=Q91088537|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

The auxiliary shrine outside the tamagaki (sacred fence) of the main hall is recorded in the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704}} as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Ōnamuji no Mikoto."

The Hachiōji Shrine on the hill to the left has a natural stone as its sacred object, and the same Meisaichō records it as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Yahashira no Kami"<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

On the hamayuka (floor platform) in front of the main hall doors, there are clay komainu (guardian dogs), which are rare in this area.

'''Mikushi Shrine''' (みくしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Fushihara, {{ill|lt=Heguri|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790}}, {{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine ranking|1=UNKNOWN}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}.

=== Overview ===
From the settlement of Ōaza Fushihara (ふしはら), if one climbs to the end of Dō no Tani (Hall Valley), the shrine is enshrined facing south on a {{ill|lt=hill|Q222912|WD=Q222912}} on the right side near the summit.

It is an ancient shrine also mentioned in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}, but the origins and history of its founding are not clear.

However, there is a theory that, based on its location along a valley behind the village, it was enshrined as a deity protecting agricultural water.

The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100}} is {{ill|lt=Ame no Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame no Koyane}}.

However, in the Nihon Sōkoku Fudoki, the name {{ill|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime}} appears, and in the {{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō}}, the name {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime}} appears, suggesting that the principal deity changed during the medieval period.

The auxiliary shrine on the right side of the grounds is dedicated to {{ill|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|Q91088537|WD=Q91088537|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

The auxiliary shrine outside the tamagaki (sacred fence) of the main hall is recorded in the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704}} as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Ōnamuji no Mikoto."

The Hachiōji Shrine on the hill to the left has a natural stone as its sacred object, and the same Meisaichō records it as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Yahashira no Kami"<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

On the hamayuka (floor platform) in front of the main hall doors, there are clay komainu (guardian dogs), which are rare in this area.

'''Mikushi Shrine''' (みくしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Fushihara, {{ill|lt=Heguri|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790}}, {{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine ranking|1=UNKNOWN}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}.

=== Overview ===
From the settlement of Ōaza Fushihara (ふしはら), if one climbs to the end of Dō no Tani (Hall Valley), the shrine is enshrined facing south on a {{ill|lt=hill|Q222912|WD=Q222912}} on the right side near the summit.

It is an ancient shrine also mentioned in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}, but the origins and history of its founding are not clear.

However, there is a theory that, based on its location along a valley behind the village, it was enshrined as a deity protecting agricultural water.

The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100}} is {{ill|lt=Ame no Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame no Koyane}}.

However, in the Nihon Sōkoku Fudoki, the name {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime}} appears, and in the {{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō}}, the name {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime}} appears, suggesting that the principal deity changed during the medieval period.

The auxiliary shrine on the right side of the grounds is dedicated to {{ill|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|Q91088537|WD=Q91088537|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

The auxiliary shrine outside the tamagaki (sacred fence) of the main hall is recorded in the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704}} as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Ōnamuji no Mikoto."

The Hachiōji Shrine on the hill to the left has a natural stone as its sacred object, and the same Meisaichō records it as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Yahashira no Kami"<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

On the hamayuka (floor platform) in front of the main hall doors, there are clay komainu (guardian dogs), which are rare in this area.

'''Mikushi Shrine''' (みくしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Fushihara, {{ill|lt=Heguri|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790}}, {{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine ranking|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}.

=== Overview ===
From the settlement of Ōaza Fushihara (ふしはら), if one climbs to the end of Dō no Tani (Hall Valley), the shrine is enshrined facing south on a {{ill|lt=hill|Q222912|WD=Q222912}} on the right side near the summit.

It is an ancient shrine also mentioned in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}, but the origins and history of its founding are not clear.

However, there is a theory that, based on its location along a valley behind the village, it was enshrined as a deity protecting agricultural water.

The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100}} is {{ill|lt=Ame no Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame no Koyane}}.

However, in the Nihon Sōkoku Fudoki, the name {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime}} appears, and in the {{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō}}, the name {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime}} appears, suggesting that the principal deity changed during the medieval period.

The auxiliary shrine on the right side of the grounds is dedicated to {{ill|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|Q91088537|WD=Q91088537|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

The auxiliary shrine outside the tamagaki (sacred fence) of the main hall is recorded in the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704}} as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Ōnamuji no Mikoto."

The Hachiōji Shrine on the hill to the left has a natural stone as its sacred object, and the same Meisaichō records it as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Yahashira no Kami"<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

On the hamayuka (floor platform) in front of the main hall doors, there are clay komainu (guardian dogs), which are rare in this area.

=== Notes ===

{{nihongo|'''Mikushi Shrine'''|御櫛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社

'''Mikushi Shrine''' (みくしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Fushihara, {{ill|lt=Heguri|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790}}, {{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine ranking|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}.

=== Overview ===
From the settlement of Ōaza Fushihara (ふしはら), if one climbs to the end of Dō no Tani (Hall Valley), the shrine is enshrined facing south on a {{ill|lt=hill|Q222912|WD=Q222912}} on the right side near the summit.

It is an ancient shrine also mentioned in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}, but the origins and history of its founding are not clear.

However, there is a theory that, based on its location along a valley behind the village, it was enshrined as a deity protecting agricultural water.

The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100}} is {{ill|lt=Ame no Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame no Koyane}}.

However, in the Nihon Sōkoku Fudoki, the name {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime}} appears, and in the {{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō}}, the name {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime}} appears, suggesting that the principal deity changed during the medieval period.

The auxiliary shrine on the right side of the grounds is dedicated to {{ill|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|Q91088537|WD=Q91088537|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

The auxiliary shrine outside the tamagaki (sacred fence) of the main hall is recorded in the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704}} as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Ōnamuji no Mikoto."

The Hachiōji Shrine on the hill to the left has a natural stone as its sacred object, and the same Meisaichō records it as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Yahashira no Kami"<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

On the hamayuka (floor platform) in front of the main hall doors, there are clay komainu (guardian dogs), which are rare in this area.

=== Notes ===

'''Mikushi Shrine''' (みくしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Fushihara, {{ill|lt=Heguri|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}.

=== Overview ===
From the settlement of Ōaza Fushihara (ふしはら), if one climbs to the end of Dō no Tani (Hall Valley), the shrine is enshrined facing south on a {{ill|lt=hill|Q222912|WD=Q222912|1=Hügelland}} on the right side near the summit.

It is an ancient shrine also mentioned in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, but the origins and history of its founding are not clear.

However, there is a theory that, based on its location along a valley behind the village, it was enshrined as a deity protecting agricultural water.

The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|lt=Ame no Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame no Koyane|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}.

However, in the Nihon Sōkoku Fudoki, the name {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|1=Toyotama-hime}} appears, and in the {{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}, the name {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime|1=Tamakushi-hime}} appears, suggesting that the principal deity changed during the medieval period.

The auxiliary shrine on the right side of the grounds is dedicated to {{ill|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|Q91088537|WD=Q91088537|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Oshikumone}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

The auxiliary shrine outside the tamagaki (sacred fence) of the main hall is recorded in the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}} as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Ōnamuji no Mikoto."

The Hachiōji Shrine on the hill to the left has a natural stone as its sacred object, and the same Meisaichō records it as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Yahashira no Kami"<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

On the hamayuka (floor platform) in front of the main hall doors, there are clay komainu (guardian dogs), which are rare in this area.

The auxiliary shrine outside the tamagaki (sacred fence) of the main hall is recorded in the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}} as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Ōnamuji no Mikoto."

The Hachiōji Shrine on the hill to the left has a natural stone as its sacred object, and the same Meisaichō records it as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Yahashira no Kami"<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

On the hamayuka (floor platform) in front of the main hall doors, there are clay komainu (guardian dogs), which are rare in this area.

'''Mikushi Shrine''' (みくしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Fushihara, {{ill|lt=Heguri|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}.

=== Overview ===
From the settlement of Ōaza Fushihara (ふしはら), if one climbs to the end of Dō no Tani (Hall Valley), the shrine is enshrined facing south on a {{ill|lt=hill|Q222912|WD=Q222912|1=Hügelland}} on the right side near the summit.

It is an ancient shrine also mentioned in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, but the origins and history of its founding are not clear.

However, there is a theory that, based on its location along a valley behind the village, it was enshrined as a deity protecting agricultural water.

The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|lt=Ame no Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame no Koyane|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}.

However, in the Nihon Sōkoku Fudoki, the name {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|1=Toyotama-hime}} appears, and in the {{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}, the name {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime|1=Tamakushi-hime}} appears, suggesting that the principal deity changed during the medieval period.

The auxiliary shrine on the right side of the grounds is dedicated to {{ill|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|Q91088537|WD=Q91088537|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Oshikumone}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

The auxiliary shrine outside the tamagaki (sacred fence) of the main hall is recorded in the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}} as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Ōnamuji no Mikoto."

The Hachiōji Shrine on the hill to the left has a natural stone as its sacred object, and the same Meisaichō records it as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Yahashira no Kami"<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

On the hamayuka (floor platform) in front of the main hall doors, there are clay komainu (guardian dogs), which are rare in this area.

'''Mikushi Shrine''' (みくしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Fushihara, {{ill|lt=Heguri|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}.

=== Overview ===
From the settlement of Ōaza Fushihara (ふしはら), if one climbs to the end of Dō no Tani (Hall Valley), the shrine is enshrined facing south on a {{ill|lt=hill|Q222912|WD=Q222912|1=Hügelland}} on the right side near the summit.

It is an ancient shrine also mentioned in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, but the origins and history of its founding are not clear.

However, there is a theory that, based on its location along a valley behind the village, it was enshrined as a deity protecting agricultural water.

The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|lt=Ame no Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame no Koyane|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}.

However, in the Nihon Sōkoku Fudoki, the name {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|1=Toyotama-hime}} appears, and in the {{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}, the name {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime|1=Tamakushi-hime}} appears, suggesting that the principal deity changed during the medieval period.

The auxiliary shrine on the right side of the grounds is dedicated to {{ill|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|Q91088537|WD=Q91088537|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Oshikumone}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

The auxiliary shrine outside the tamagaki (sacred fence) of the main hall is recorded in the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}} as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Ōnamuji no Mikoto."

The Hachiōji Shrine on the hill to the left has a natural stone as its sacred object, and the same Meisaichō records it as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Yahashira no Kami"<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

On the hamayuka (floor platform) in front of the main hall doors, there are clay komainu (guardian dogs), which are rare in this area.

'''Mikushi Shrine''' (みくしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Fushihara, {{ill|lt=Heguri|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}.

=== Overview ===
From the settlement of Ōaza Fushihara (ふしはら), if one climbs to the end of Dō no Tani (Hall Valley), the shrine is enshrined facing south on a {{ill|lt=hill|Q222912|WD=Q222912|1=Hügelland}} on the right side near the summit.

It is an ancient shrine also mentioned in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, but the origins and history of its founding are not clear.

However, there is a theory that, based on its location along a valley behind the village, it was enshrined as a deity protecting agricultural water.

The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|lt=Ame no Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame no Koyane|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}.

However, in the Nihon Sōkoku Fudoki, the name {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|1=Toyotama-hime}} appears, and in the {{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}, the name {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime|1=Tamakushi-hime}} appears, suggesting that the principal deity changed during the medieval period.

The auxiliary shrine on the right side of the grounds is dedicated to {{ill|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|Q91088537|WD=Q91088537|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Oshikumone}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

The auxiliary shrine outside the tamagaki (sacred fence) of the main hall is recorded in the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}} as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Ōnamuji no Mikoto."

The Hachiōji Shrine on the hill to the left has a natural stone as its sacred object, and the same Meisaichō records it as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Yahashira no Kami"<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

On the hamayuka (floor platform) in front of the main hall doors, there are clay komainu (guardian dogs), which are rare in this area.

{{nihongo|'''Mikushi Shrine'''|御櫛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Mikushi Shrine''' (みくしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Fushihara, {{ill|lt=Heguri|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}.

=== Overview ===
From the settlement of Ōaza Fushihara (ふしはら), if one climbs to the end of Dō no Tani (Hall Valley), the shrine is enshrined facing south on a {{ill|lt=hill|Q222912|WD=Q222912|1=Hügelland}} on the right side near the summit.

It is an ancient shrine also mentioned in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, but the origins and history of its founding are not clear.

However, there is a theory that, based on its location along a valley behind the village, it was enshrined as a deity protecting agricultural water.

The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|lt=Ame no Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame no Koyane|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}.

However, in the Nihon Sōkoku Fudoki, the name {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|1=Toyotama-hime}} appears, and in the {{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}, the name {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime|1=Tamakushi-hime}} appears, suggesting that the principal deity changed during the medieval period.

The auxiliary shrine on the right side of the grounds is dedicated to {{ill|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|Q91088537|WD=Q91088537|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Oshikumone}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

The auxiliary shrine outside the tamagaki (sacred fence) of the main hall is recorded in the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}} as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Ōnamuji no Mikoto."

The Hachiōji Shrine on the hill to the left has a natural stone as its sacred object, and the same Meisaichō records it as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Yahashira no Kami"<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

On the hamayuka (floor platform) in front of the main hall doors, there are clay komainu (guardian dogs), which are rare in this area.

'''Mikushi Shrine''' (みくしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Fushihara, {{ill|lt=Heguri|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}.

=== Overview ===
From the settlement of Ōaza Fushihara (ふしはら), if one climbs to the end of Dō no Tani (Hall Valley), the shrine is enshrined facing south on a {{ill|lt=hill|Q222912|WD=Q222912|1=Hügelland}} on the right side near the summit.

It is an ancient shrine also mentioned in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, but the origins and history of its founding are not clear.

However, there is a theory that, based on its location along a valley behind the village, it was enshrined as a deity protecting agricultural water.

The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|lt=Ame no Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame no Koyane|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}.

However, in the Nihon Sōkoku Fudoki, the name {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|1=Toyotama-hime}} appears, and in the {{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}, the name {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime|1=Tamakushi-hime}} appears, suggesting that the principal deity changed during the medieval period.

The auxiliary shrine on the right side of the grounds is dedicated to {{ill|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|Q91088537|WD=Q91088537|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Oshikumone}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

The auxiliary shrine outside the tamagaki (sacred fence) of the main hall is recorded in the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}} as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Ōnamuji no Mikoto."

The Hachiōji Shrine on the hill to the left has a natural stone as its sacred object, and the same Meisaichō records it as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Yahashira no Kami"<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

On the hamayuka (floor platform) in front of the main hall doors, there are clay komainu (guardian dogs), which are rare in this area.

{{nihongo|'''Mikushi Shrine'''|御櫛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Mikushi Shrine''' (みくしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Fushihara, {{ill|lt=Heguri|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}.

=== Overview ===
From the settlement of Ōaza Fushihara (ふしはら), if one climbs to the end of Dō no Tani (Hall Valley), the shrine is enshrined facing south on a {{ill|lt=hill|Q222912|WD=Q222912|1=Hügelland}} on the right side near the summit.

It is an ancient shrine also mentioned in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, but the origins and history of its founding are not clear.

However, there is a theory that, based on its location along a valley behind the village, it was enshrined as a deity protecting agricultural water.

The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|lt=Ame no Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame no Koyane|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}.

However, in the Nihon Sōkoku Fudoki, the name {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|1=Toyotama-hime}} appears, and in the {{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}, the name {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime|1=Tamakushi-hime}} appears, suggesting that the principal deity changed during the medieval period.

The auxiliary shrine on the right side of the grounds is dedicated to {{ill|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|Q91088537|WD=Q91088537|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Oshikumone}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

The auxiliary shrine outside the tamagaki (sacred fence) of the main hall is recorded in the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}} as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Ōnamuji no Mikoto."

The Hachiōji Shrine on the hill to the left has a natural stone as its sacred object, and the same Meisaichō records it as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Yahashira no Kami"<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

On the hamayuka (floor platform) in front of the main hall doors, there are clay komainu (guardian dogs), which are rare in this area.

{{nihongo|'''Mikushi Shrine'''|御櫛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Mikushi Shrine''' (みくしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Fushihara, {{ill|lt=Heguri|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}.

=== Overview ===
From the settlement of Ōaza Fushihara (ふしはら), if one climbs to the end of Dō no Tani (Hall Valley), the shrine is enshrined facing south on a {{ill|lt=hill|Q222912|WD=Q222912|1=Hügelland}} on the right side near the summit.

It is an ancient shrine also mentioned in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, but the origins and history of its founding are not clear.

However, there is a theory that, based on its location along a valley behind the village, it was enshrined as a deity protecting agricultural water.

The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|lt=Ame no Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame no Koyane|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}.

However, in the Nihon Sōkoku Fudoki, the name {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|1=Toyotama-hime}} appears, and in the {{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}, the name {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime|1=Tamakushi-hime}} appears, suggesting that the principal deity changed during the medieval period.

The auxiliary shrine on the right side of the grounds is dedicated to {{ill|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|Q91088537|WD=Q91088537|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Oshikumone}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

The auxiliary shrine outside the tamagaki (sacred fence) of the main hall is recorded in the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}} as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Ōnamuji no Mikoto."

The Hachiōji Shrine on the hill to the left has a natural stone as its sacred object, and the same Meisaichō records it as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Yahashira no Kami"<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

On the hamayuka (floor platform) in front of the main hall doors, there are clay komainu (guardian dogs), which are rare in this area.

'''Mikushi Shrine''' (みくしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Fushihara, {{ill|lt=Heguri|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}.

=== Overview ===
From the settlement of Ōaza Fushihara (ふしはら), if one climbs to the end of Dō no Tani (Hall Valley), the shrine is enshrined facing south on a {{ill|lt=hill|Q222912|WD=Q222912|1=Hügelland}} on the right side near the summit.

It is an ancient shrine also mentioned in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, but the origins and history of its founding are not clear.

However, there is a theory that, based on its location along a valley behind the village, it was enshrined as a deity protecting agricultural water.

The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|lt=Ame no Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame no Koyane|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}.

However, in the Nihon Sōkoku Fudoki, the name {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|1=Toyotama-hime}} appears, and in the {{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}, the name {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime|1=Tamakushi-hime}} appears, suggesting that the principal deity changed during the medieval period.

The auxiliary shrine on the right side of the grounds is dedicated to {{ill|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|Q91088537|WD=Q91088537|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Oshikumone}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

The auxiliary shrine outside the tamagaki (sacred fence) of the main hall is recorded in the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}} as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Ōnamuji no Mikoto."

The Hachiōji Shrine on the hill to the left has a natural stone as its sacred object, and the same Meisaichō records it as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Yahashira no Kami"<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

On the hamayuka (floor platform) in front of the main hall doors, there are clay komainu (guardian dogs), which are rare in this area.

'''Mikushi Shrine''' (みくしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Fushihara, {{ill|lt=Heguri|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}.

=== Overview ===
From the settlement of Ōaza Fushihara (ふしはら), if one climbs to the end of Dō no Tani (Hall Valley), the shrine is enshrined facing south on a {{ill|lt=hill|Q222912|WD=Q222912|1=Hügelland}} on the right side near the summit.

It is an ancient shrine also mentioned in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, but the origins and history of its founding are not clear.

However, there is a theory that, based on its location along a valley behind the village, it was enshrined as a deity protecting agricultural water.

The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|lt=Ame no Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame no Koyane|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}.

However, in the Nihon Sōkoku Fudoki, the name {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|1=Toyotama-hime}} appears, and in the {{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}, the name {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime|1=Tamakushi-hime}} appears, suggesting that the principal deity changed during the medieval period.

The auxiliary shrine on the right side of the grounds is dedicated to {{ill|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|Q91088537|WD=Q91088537|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Oshikumone}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

The auxiliary shrine outside the tamagaki (sacred fence) of the main hall is recorded in the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}} as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Ōnamuji no Mikoto."

The Hachiōji Shrine on the hill to the left has a natural stone as its sacred object, and the same Meisaichō records it as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Yahashira no Kami"<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

On the hamayuka (floor platform) in front of the main hall doors, there are clay komainu (guardian dogs), which are rare in this area.

'''Mikushi Shrine''' (みくしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Fushihara, {{ill|lt=Heguri|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}.

=== Overview ===
From the settlement of Ōaza Fushihara (ふしはら), if one climbs to the end of Dō no Tani (Hall Valley), the shrine is enshrined facing south on a {{ill|lt=hill|Q222912|WD=Q222912|1=Hügelland}} on the right side near the summit.

It is an ancient shrine also mentioned in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, but the origins and history of its founding are not clear.

However, there is a theory that, based on its location along a valley behind the village, it was enshrined as a deity protecting agricultural water.

The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|lt=Ame no Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame no Koyane|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}.

However, in the Nihon Sōkoku Fudoki, the name {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|1=Toyotama-hime}} appears, and in the {{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}, the name {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime|1=Tamakushi-hime}} appears, suggesting that the principal deity changed during the medieval period.

The auxiliary shrine on the right side of the grounds is dedicated to {{ill|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|Q91088537|WD=Q91088537|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Oshikumone}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

The auxiliary shrine outside the tamagaki (sacred fence) of the main hall is recorded in the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}} as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Ōnamuji no Mikoto."

The Hachiōji Shrine on the hill to the left has a natural stone as its sacred object, and the same Meisaichō records it as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Yahashira no Kami"<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

On the hamayuka (floor platform) in front of the main hall doors, there are clay komainu (guardian dogs), which are rare in this area.

'''Mikushi Shrine''' (みくしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Fushihara, {{ill|lt=Heguri|Q1346790|qid=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|qid=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}.

=== Overview ===
From the settlement of Ōaza Fushihara (ふしはら), if one climbs to the end of Dō no Tani (Hall Valley), the shrine is enshrined facing south on a {{ill|lt=hill|Q222912|qid=Q222912|1=Hügelland}} on the right side near the summit.

It is an ancient shrine also mentioned in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, but the origins and history of its founding are not clear.

However, there is a theory that, based on its location along a valley behind the village, it was enshrined as a deity protecting agricultural water.

The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|qid=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|lt=Ame no Koyane|Q2564508|qid=Q2564508|lt=Ame no Koyane|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}.

However, in the Nihon Sōkoku Fudoki, the name {{ill|qid=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|qid=Q2329261|qid=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|1=Toyotama-hime}} appears, and in the {{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|Q11571092|qid=Q11571092|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}, the name {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|qid=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime|1=Tamakushi-hime}} appears, suggesting that the principal deity changed during the medieval period.

The auxiliary shrine on the right side of the grounds is dedicated to {{ill|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|Q91088537|qid=Q91088537|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Oshikumone}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

The auxiliary shrine outside the tamagaki (sacred fence) of the main hall is recorded in the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|qid=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}} as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Ōnamuji no Mikoto."

The Hachiōji Shrine on the hill to the left has a natural stone as its sacred object, and the same Meisaichō records it as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Yahashira no Kami"<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

On the hamayuka (floor platform) in front of the main hall doors, there are clay komainu (guardian dogs), which are rare in this area.

'''Mikushi Shrine''' (みくしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Fushihara, {{ill|lt=Heguri|Q1346790|qid=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|qid=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}.

=== Overview ===
From the settlement of Ōaza Fushihara (ふしはら), if one climbs to the end of Dō no Tani (Hall Valley), the shrine is enshrined facing south on a {{ill|lt=hill|Q222912|qid=Q222912|1=Hügelland}} on the right side near the summit.

It is an ancient shrine also mentioned in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, but the origins and history of its founding are not clear.

However, there is a theory that, based on its location along a valley behind the village, it was enshrined as a deity protecting agricultural water.

The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|qid=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|lt=Ame no Koyane|Q2564508|qid=Q2564508|lt=Ame no Koyane|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}.

However, in the Nihon Sōkoku Fudoki, the name {{ill|qid=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|qid=Q2329261|qid=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|1=Toyotama-hime}} appears, and in the {{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|Q11571092|qid=Q11571092|lt=Tokusen Shinmyōchō|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}, the name {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|qid=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime|1=Tamakushi-hime}} appears, suggesting that the principal deity changed during the medieval period.

The auxiliary shrine on the right side of the grounds is dedicated to {{ill|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|Q91088537|qid=Q91088537|lt=Ame no Oshikumone no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Oshikumone}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

The auxiliary shrine outside the tamagaki (sacred fence) of the main hall is recorded in the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|qid=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}} as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Ōnamuji no Mikoto."

The Hachiōji Shrine on the hill to the left has a natural stone as its sacred object, and the same Meisaichō records it as "enshrined deity unknown, or possibly Yahashira no Kami"<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=665-667}}</ref>.

On the hamayuka (floor platform) in front of the main hall doors, there are clay komainu (guardian dogs), which are rare in this area.
